revisions.comment: /* Genocide */ Altered url. URLs might have been anonymized.

Removed "with the stated goal of regime change." Is it necessary to include motive for this partly-related conflict?

1 line in the body about this and a few month study ending in April 2024 that looked at a narrowed criteria ('residential buildings') is [[WP:UNDUE]] for the lead. Lead summarizes overall effects of war, not strip of time over 2 years ago.

The body cites different figures and claims and the sources are from Fall 2024 making it [[WP:UNDUE]]. Also we have not cited that the majority of Israeli deaths from Oct 7 were civilians (around 2/3 according to the article) which we'd then have to per [[WP:DUE]] and [[WP:NPOV]].
/* Casualties */ why is this sentence restated word for word and with the same sources twice in one section?

/* Casualties */ trim section to enforce [[WP:TOOBIG]] size guidelines in line with rest of article

Correcting a tense shift: 'estimated', 'undercounted', 'noting -> noted'. Grammatical issue: you don't estimate that there is an undercount, you conclude or assess that there is an undercount. Finally 'higher death toll' is more appropriate that 'larger death toll'.

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1339758577|1339758577]] by [[Special:Contributions/Davefelmer|Davefelmer]] ([[User talk:Davefelmer|talk]]) This is long-standing material in the lede (going back at least 2000 revisions, beyond which I stopped counting). Three editors have now directly contested your removal; a fourth has suggested you should self-revert. As that is not forthcoming, and you are now engaging in personal attacks on the talk page, I see no reason to wait further.
Not in cited sources, also obfuscates ratio that is clarified in sources in the article

Every change doesn't need talk consensus. This is a simple case of [[WP:UNDUE]] per [[WP:LEADFOLLOWSBODY]]. Being covered in 1 paragraph out of 8 in one section of 9 main multi-paragraph body sections is not prominent enough for this one study to be included in the lead. These are basic project rules

This is [[WP:OR]]. I see one line in the body under the Casualties section noting Israel has killed scores of journalists and academics, and maybe 1-3 mentions elsewhere that Israel killed medics or journalists during an airstrike. That's nowhere close to enough or prominent enough for what's been added as a standalone piece, merge mention into previous sentence describing overall destruction.
per [[Talk:Gaza war#Gaza genocide in the first paragraph]], restoring mention and link to [[Gaza genocide]] in first paragraph that was removed by user:Davefelmer against consensus

merge two overlapping sections, "reactions", which was titled the very first week of the conflict, and "diplomatic impact", which is more appropriate for the legacy of a two-year war. remove duplication with other sections, outdated [[WP:RECENTISM]], and excessive detail, in order to enforce [[WP:TOOBIG]] and [[WP:SS]].
